channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. stock futures mixed as Mideast tensions rise; South Korea's Kospi plunges 7%: Live updates
item.description: Investors will also be focused on inflation data and the public debut of SpaceX on Friday.

item.title: Trump storms out of interview after being challenged about election fraud claims, DOJ fund
item.description: The president said he would like to see the weaponization fund proceed despite setbacks.

item.title: Foreign investors have dumped billions of dollars of Korean stocks this year despite record rally. Here's why
item.description: The selling intensified Monday as the benchmark Kospi plunged more than 8% at the open, even as it emerged as one of the world's standout performers thus far.

item.title: Oil prices spike over 3% as Iran and Israel trade strikes, escalating regional tensions
item.description: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.

item.title: Asia tech stocks extend sell-off with SoftBank down over 7% as investors sour on AI-linked names
item.description: Asian tech stocks tracked overnight losses in U.S. tech names led by a sell-off in Broadcom.

item.title: 'Dean of Valuation' Aswath Damodaran is not buying SpaceX: 'Too richly priced'
item.description: The NYU professor said he understands why some people are getting ready to pay up for SpaceX, but he is "more investor than trader."

item.title: Why Nokia missed the smartphone shift
item.description: For years, Nokia didn't just compete in mobile phones — it defined the category. From the iconic 3310 to the best-selling Nokia 1100, the Finnish company built a global empire on durable devices, mass-market appeal and an unmatched ability to scale. At its peak, Nokia was Europe's most valuable company and one of the most dominant consumer tech brands in the world. But the smartphone era changed where value in the industry was created.

item.title: 'Bring 'em on': Delta wants United's crown over the Pacific, too
item.description: Delta's president, Peter Carter, told CNBC that the carrier wants to take United on over the Pacific.

item.title: Women often outlive men. Here's how they should approach their long-term care needs
item.description: In addition to typically living longer than their husbands, women also are more likely to need long-term care, which can be costly.

item.title: These are the most overbought stocks after a volatile week
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise was among this week's most overbought stocks.

item.title: 100 days of the Iran war: How global markets and the economy have been affected, in charts
item.description: The conflict is about to reach its 100th day, with peace negotiations hanging in the balance.

item.title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
item.description: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.

item.title: Remote work career risks for Gen Z are 'subtle, but material,' Harvard professor says: How to avoid the pitfalls
item.description: Forgoing the office can have detrimental "scarring" impacts on young workers. Here's how to navigate the benefits and drawbacks of remote work.

item.title: Hong Kong's IPO boom is developing a performance problem
item.description: As Hong Kong vies with Wall Street to be the top IPO market, a growing number of pre-debut runups turn sour after their listing.

item.title: OPEC+ approves fourth oil output quota hike since Hormuz closure
item.description: Seven members of OPEC+, which groups OPEC and allied producers including Russia, increased output quotas from April to June by almost 600,000 barrels per day.

item.title: Earthquake of magnitude 7.8 strikes off southern Philippines, tsunami warnings issued
item.description: Tsunami warnings were issued after an earthquake of magnitude 7.8 struck off Mindanao in the southern Philippines on Monday.

item.title: Why AI hyperscalers are now the epicenter of a bear case for stocks
item.description: In the near term, there are many mouths to feed and not enough to eat.

item.title: Inflation inside the electronics you buy may soon become a bit more sticky
item.description: Most consumers are not familiar with the role resin plays inside electronics like smartphones. A current shortage of the materials may push up prices.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties at Madison Square Garden with Trump attending Game 3 of the NBA Finals
item.description: Trump is a longtime Knicks fan who confirmed Friday that he would attend the first NBA Finals game in New York since 1999.

item.title: Inside the Obama-era Iran nuclear deal that Trump withdrew from
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have yet to reach a peace deal or address Iran's nuclear ambitions, despite signals from Trump that talks are progressing.

item.title: Here are the 6 big things we're watching in the stock market this week
item.description: Earnings season for Club names has come to an end, but that doesn't mean the excitement has.

item.title: Best mortgage lenders for bad credit in June 2026
item.description: Having less-than-perfect credit doesn't have to be an obstacle to homeownership.

item.title: Harry's and Coterie owner Mammoth Brands has ambitions to be the next CPG giant
item.description: Mammoth's direct-to-consumer brands have helped upend the razor, diaper and deodorant categories.

item.title: Global week ahead: Soccer isn't the only thing that's kicking off
item.description: World Cup fever grips football fans worldwide ahead of the opening game on June 11th, but it's not just soccer that's kicking off next week.

item.title: Pope Leo tells 1.2 million crowd in Madrid that God stands with the poor
item.description: Leo said he hoped the visit would set an example to the world about respecting "every human being" and urged leaders to stop dividing electorates.

item.title: Top Wall Street analysts recommend these 3 dividend stocks for solid returns
item.description: Investors seeking steady income can bolster their portfolios by adding dividend stocks with attractive yields.

item.title: Nvidia, SK to detail cooperation plan as Huang flags prolonged chip shortage
item.description: A SK Hynix spokesperson said group Chairman Chey Tae-won and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang plan to brief the media on Monday morning, confirming a report by Newsis.

item.title: Chinese EVs may hit U.S. within a few years, one way or another
item.description: Chinese electric vehicles are likely to be sold in the U.S. within years, despite tariffs, strict laws and opposition from the auto industry and politicians.

item.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
item.description: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.

item.title: Banco BPM invites MPS to $58 billion merger talks to form Italy's second-biggest bank
item.description: The announcement could kick-off a second wave of dealmaking in Italian banking after a burst of M&A activity last year.
